notes: Primary key. decimal(10,0) carries no fractional part; INT64 is the lossless BigQuery equivalent and avoids unnecessary NUMERIC overhead.
varchar(50) maps directly to BigQuery STRING; no length constraint enforced at rest in BigQuery.
varchar(50) maps directly to BigQuery STRING.
varchar(100) maps directly to BigQuery STRING.
Nullable in source; stored as STRING to preserve non-numeric characters such as plus signs, parentheses, and dashes.
MySQL DATE maps directly to BigQuery DATE with no precision loss.
Second clustering column; job-role-scoped analytics are a common access pattern for HR reporting.
DECIMAL(8,2) requires 2 decimal places; BigQuery NUMERIC supports up to 29 digits with 9 decimal places, preserving precision without rounding.
Nullable in source; DECIMAL(4,2) retains 2 decimal fractional digits; NUMERIC preserves this precision losslessly.
Self-referencing FK; decimal(10,0) has no fractional part so INT64 is the appropriate lossless mapping. Nullable for root-level managers.
Leading clustering column; department-scoped filtering is the primary analytical access pattern for HR workloads on this table.
Third clustering column; low cardinality makes it highly effective for BigQuery block elimination when filtering active vs. inactive employees.
DAY-level partition column; aligns with the hourly batch schedule so each run writes to at most one or two partitions, enabling efficient partition pruning and incremental overwrite patterns.
Nullable; carries the source-system last-modified timestamp and is the recommended watermark column for downstream incremental MERGE operations.
